title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation · Runtime Lens: Count Complexity
summary: A counting loop resets existing entries, aliases a shared dictionary, includes empty tokens, sorts while mutating, and never reconciles token totals with frequency totals. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the count complexity increment in inspectable frequency engine. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Derive expected time and space cost from tokens and vocabulary before measuring implementation behavior.
Merge partial counters associatively and test partition independence for streaming and parallel transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Bookbot team handles case 6445 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive expected time and space cost from tokens and vocabulary before measuring implementation behavior. A defensible solution applies Count Complexity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to inspectable frequency engine.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_frequency_counter_2(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-count-complexity-1h1xwh
# Competency: bookbot-count-complexity.
# Case 4138: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_count_complexity_1h1xwh(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1h1xwh = "1h1xwh-hwx1h1-4138"
    assert evidence_variant_1h1xwh
    evidence_axes_1h1xwh = (
        "corpus1h1xwh",
        "policyhwx1h1",
        "revision4138",
        "command1h1xwh4138",
        "reporthwx1h14138",
        "test41381h1xwh",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1h1xwh)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"
